title: SSGM Governance Patterns
description: Stability and Safety-Governed Memory (SSGM) tiers and synchronization protocols.

axiomAlignment.A1_verifiability: T1 Ledger is the immutable source of truth for all cognitive operations.
axiomAlignment.A3_transparency: Clear separation between ephemeral working memory (T3) and relational indexing (T2).
axiomAlignment.A5_consistency: Unified sync scripts ensure all tiers remain reconciled.

three_tier_architecture.description: The standard architectural split for agent memory.
three_tier_architecture.use_when: Designing new memory-intensive features or troubleshooting context issues.
three_tier_architecture.code_example:
Tier 1: Filesystem (.agent/)
Tier 2: Relational (Memory MCP)
Tier 3: Working (Brain/Artifacts)
best_practices: Never store permanent rules in Tier 3
Regularly reconcile Tier 2 from Tier 1 truth
Keep Tier 3 as concise as possible to avoid context window flooding

schema_validation.description: Ensuring all SSGM T1 assets comply with their respective JSON schemas.
best_practices: Validate before commit using automated test suites.
Ensure ID consistency between filename and metadata.

name: SSGM Governance Patterns
best_practices: Index all newly discovered scripts, workflows, and skills immediately.
Include purpose, goal, and keywords in memory observations.
Link KIs to the tasks where they were synthesized.
anti_patterns: Manual memory entry without verifying filesystem existence.
Stale entries that conflict with the actual code in scripts/.
